description_long: This object consists of two distinct swords placed side by side, each with a unique design. The left sword features a long, straight, blue-tinted blade with a pointed tip and a decorative, geometric crossguard, while the right sword has a broader, black blade with a pronounced angular tip and a more robust, rectangular crossguard. Both swords have cylindrical handles, with the left handle ending in a rounded pommel and the right handle featuring a spherical pommel. The left sword's blade appears to have a crystalline or metallic sheen, while the right sword is matte and solid in appearance. The crossguards are visually prominent, providing both protection and aesthetic detail. These swords are likely constructed from metal, with possible plastic or rubber elements in the handles for grip.
description: A pair of visually striking swords, one with a blue crystalline blade and the other with a broad black blade, both featuring prominent crossguards and cylindrical handles.
description_view_0: Front view showing both swords side by side, highlighting the blue blade and geometric crossguard of the left sword and the broad, angular black blade of the right sword.
description_view_1: Side view showing the thin profile of both swords, emphasizing the straightness and thickness of the blades.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the blue and black blades and their crossguards visible from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the handles' shapes, with the crossguards visible in silhouette.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, smooth, with rounded and spherical pommels, minimal visible texture
attribute_ornamentation: geometric crossguard, blue crystalline blade finish
